title: LVSum: A Benchmark for Timestamp-Aware Long Video Summarization
gist: Apple's LVSum benchmark (72 videos, 13 domains, 16-min average, human timestamped summaries) shows that transcripts matter more than visual frames for long-video summarization, that a large human-vs-model quality gap persists, and that current MLLMs are systematically weak at temporal grounding.

insights.claim: In long-video summarization, transcripts contribute substantially more to summary quality than visual frames alone across leading proprietary and open-source multimodal LLMs.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Implies compute and product value in long-video AI still leans on ASR/text pipelines over pixel-level vision, shaping where to invest.
insights.quote: transcripts contribute substantially more to summarization quality than visual frames alone
insights.locator: Our experiments reveal three key findings

insights.claim: A significant performance gap persists between model-generated and human-written summaries for long-form video, indicating the task is far from solved even for frontier MLLMs.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Long-video understanding remains an open frontier, leaving headroom (and risk) for any vendor claiming production-grade video summarization.
insights.quote: a significant performance gap persists between model-generated and human-written summaries
insights.locator: Our experiments reveal three key findings

insights.claim: Current MLLMs exhibit systematic weaknesses in temporal grounding, instruction adherence, and cross-modal coherence when summarizing long videos.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Names the three failure axes to probe when evaluating any video-AI product before trusting its timestamped outputs.
insights.quote: current MLLMs exhibit systematic weaknesses in temporal grounding, instruction adherence, and cross-modal coherence
insights.locator: Our experiments reveal three key findings

insights.claim: LVSum is a human-annotated long-video benchmark of 72 videos across 13 domains averaging 16 minutes each, with up to 10 human summaries per video carrying temporal references.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: A timestamp-aware, multi-domain benchmark gives investors a concrete yardstick to compare competing video-LLM claims.
insights.quote: LVSum comprises 72 diverse videos spanning 13 domains with an average duration of 16 minutes, each annotated with up to 10 human-generated summaries containing temporal references.

insights.claim: Apple introduces LLM-based metrics for content relevance and modality coherence to evaluate summaries, going beyond standard automatic metrics for temporal grounding.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: New LLM-judge metrics for temporal fidelity signal where evaluation tooling for video AI is heading.
insights.quote: newly introduced LLM-based metrics for content relevance and modality coherence
